title: Deverra tortuosa
uses: Deverra tortuosa has been traditionally used in some parts of Africa for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various health issues. However, there is limited scientific research available on its specific uses and benefits. Some sources suggest that Deverra tortuosa may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness. The plant is also said to be used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, but these uses are not widely recognized or accepted. Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Deverra tortuosa.
taxonomy: Deverra tortuosa is classified as a species within the genus Deverra, which belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant is a member of the order Apiales. The family Apiaceae is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. The genus Deverra is native to tropical Africa and consists of several species of shrubs and trees. Deverra tortuosa is one of the species within this genus.

Additionally, Deverra tortuosa has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. More research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and confirm its effectiveness.
